IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
This work is taking place in The Ling area.
We are removing overhead electricity cables and replacing them with new underground electricity
cables. We will also be installing a new piece of equipment called a substation. Doing this will
improve the reliability of the electricity network and reduce the risk of you having an unplanned
power cut in the future.
To connect the new cables and complete the work safely we need to turn your power off. To
ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our contractors may carry out preparation
works prior to the power cut.

To connect and disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: ** These works were originally planned for the 26th of February 2026 but were cancelled **  We regularly inspect our electrical equipment to ensure it is safe and efficient. We are removing a piece of equipment from our electricity network called a pole mounted transformer and replacing the transformer with a new underground transformer, Once this is completed this will enable some overhead electricity cables to be removed. This essential work helps to provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, reducing the risk of any unplanned power cuts in the future. To keep everyone safe while we carry out these works, we will need to temporarily turn off your electricity. We are taking extra measures to limit the time your power is off and we'll do our best to cause as little disruption as possible. Doing this will improve the reliability of the electricity network in the future. This work is taking place on Newborough Road.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hello, Lisa from the messaging team here. Thanks for your patience tonight, our field staff and control room engineers are working to get your power back on as quickly as they can.

Our records show that some properties affected by today's power cut may have had other unexpected interruptions recently. If you have, we're sorry for further disruption. All power cuts are fully investigated by our engineers but can sometimes happen multiple times before we can safely find the issue and fix it.

We know that not everyone affected by today's power cut will have had other power cuts recently, but we want you to know that if you have, our customer services team will try to update as many customers as possible about our investigations.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 20:49. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Lisa at 04:48 - Thank you for your patience while our engineers connect the temporary generators to the underground network in the Horsham RH13 8 area, we've now restored power to most of the affected properties. There are now only 3 properties without power, who we'll speak to directly. If you're without power and haven't been contacted, then we'll need to hear from you. Please call us on 08003163105 (free from landlines and mobiles).

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the unexpected outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Lee at 06:45 - Our engineers are on their way. At this point we think the issue is an overhead cable fault affecting 15 properties.

Specialist engineers from our control room are also working to divert power remotely, so it's possible your power could come back on at any point. We can't always restore everyone this way. That's why we're also sending staff to the area to check our equipment.

Every fault is different, so our time frames have to be estimated but currently we hope to have power back on between 09:00 - 10:00 We must stress that it may become necessary for this to change, depending on what our engineers find.

We'll keep you updated with our progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're arranging staff to attend ASAP, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting electricity. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to get as many customers back on as we can.

Once available, our engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, they will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will work to locate the issue and make repairs, which may take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing but we'll always give you the clearest information we have.
